methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $1.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Neko Health is a Series B biotech company founded by Daniel Ek and Hjalmar Nilsonne, having raised $326M+ against a last post-money valuation of $1.8B. The valuation analysis draws from a comp universe of 3 public biotech firms (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage peers in Biotech & Health, including Eikon Therapeutics, Asimov, Profluent, and New Limit.

Two triangulation methods proved applicable. The stage-progression method, applying a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical for Series B advancement, implies a valuation of $3.4B. The peer-median approach across four same-sector, same-stage private comparables—whose valuations range from $600M to $3.3B—yields a median of $805M. Public-comp anchoring could not be applied due to absence of revenue disclosure. These two methods produce a wide range reflecting inherent dispersion in early-stage biotech valuations.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $684M (low) to $3.9B (high), with a median of $2.1B. The current $1.8B valuation sits within this range but closer to the lower bound, suggesting modest upside under stage-progression logic yet possible compression relative to peer multiples. Key uncertainties include the target's revenue trajectory (necessary for public-comp validation), clinical stage and program maturity relative to peer cohorts, and the applicability of the 1.9x multiplier given sector-specific funding dynamics.
